project.name: aris-proxy-api
project.description: A production-ready Go backend template built with modern technologies and best practices, providing a robust foundation for building RESTful APIs with authentication, database management, object storage, and more.

nodes.name: Docker-Publish
nodes.summary: Multi-architecture Docker image build and publish pipeline for GHCR, with multi-platform matrix builds, digest merging, and K8s deployment trigger.

nodes.name: Brainstorming Skill
nodes.summary: Workflow skill for brainstorming ideas into designs through structured exploration, clarifying questions, approach proposals, design validation, and spec writing.

nodes.name: Spec Document Reviewer Prompt
nodes.summary: Prompt template for dispatching a sub-agent to review spec documents for completeness, consistency, clarity, and scope before implementation planning.

nodes.name: Visual Companion Guide
nodes.summary: Guide for browser-based visual brainstorming companion that shows mockups, diagrams, and design options during collaborative design sessions.

nodes.name: Frame Template
nodes.summary: Self-contained HTML frame template for the brainstorming visual companion with OS-aware theming, A/B/C option selectors, card layouts, mockup containers, and pros/cons sections.

nodes.name: Start Server
nodes.summary: Shell script to start the brainstorm companion HTTP server with configurable bind host, project directory, and foreground/background modes, supporting cross-platform environments.

nodes.name: Stop Server
nodes.summary: Shell script to gracefully stop the brainstorm companion server with PID tracking, graceful shutdown timeout, fallback SIGKILL, and ephemeral directory cleanup.

nodes.name: Grill With Docs Skill
nodes.summary: Skill for grilling sessions that challenge plans against existing domain models, sharpen terminology, and update CONTEXT.md and ADR documentation inline as decisions crystallize.

nodes.name: ADR Format Guide
nodes.summary: Format specification for Architecture Decision Records with lightweight template, optional sections, numbering conventions, and guidelines for when to create an ADR.

nodes.name: CONTEXT Format Guide
nodes.summary: Format specification for CONTEXT.md domain glossary files with structure rules, term definitions, relationship expressions, and single-vs-multi-context repository conventions.

nodes.name: Improve Codebase Architecture Skill
nodes.summary: Skill for surfacing architectural friction and proposing deepening opportunities that turn shallow modules into deep ones, improving testability and AI-navigability.

nodes.name: Deepening Guide
nodes.summary: Guide for deepening shallow modules safely by classifying dependencies into in-process, local-substitutable, remote-but-owned, and true external categories with seam discipline and testing strategy.

nodes.name: HTML Report Format Guide
nodes.summary: Format specification for self-contained HTML architecture review reports using Tailwind and Mermaid, with candidate cards, before/after diagrams, and editorial styling guidance.

nodes.name: Interface Design Guide
nodes.summary: Guide for exploring alternative interface designs for deepened modules through parallel sub-agent exploration with different design constraints and comparative evaluation.

nodes.name: Architecture Language Glossary
nodes.summary: Shared vocabulary defining module, interface, implementation, depth, seam, adapter, leverage, and locality for consistent architectural communication across all suggestions.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Frontend agent rules for web directory. Declares that this project uses a version of Next.js with breaking changes and instructs agents to read the relevant guide in node_modules before writing code.

sections.heading: This is NOT the Next.js you know

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Frontend Claude rules. References AGENTS.md via @include directive, serving as an alias for the main agent rules file.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui component configuration. Defines style (base-nova), aliases for imports, icon library (lucide-react), and registry URLs for the base-nova theme.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend npm package configuration. Defines scripts (dev, build, lint), dependencies including Next.js 16.2.6, React 19, shadcn/ui, Tailwind v4, and devDependencies for TypeScript and ESLint.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for the frontend. Sets ES2022 target, strict mode, JSX preserve, path aliases (@/ mapping to src/), and includes/excludes for the Next.js project.

nodes.name: helper.js
nodes.summary: Client-side helper script for brainstorming skill hot-reload. Provides WebSocket connection management, event queuing, and page reload on content changes.

nodes.name: server.cjs
nodes.summary: Node.js development server for brainstorming skill. Implements a raw WebSocket server with HTTP fallback, file watching with debouncing, lifecycle management, and screenshot content serving.

nodes.name: Dispatching Parallel Agents
nodes.summary: Skill guide for dispatching parallel agents. Covers when and how to identify independent domains, create focused agent tasks, dispatch in parallel, and review/integrate results.

nodes.name: Executing Plans
nodes.summary: Skill guide for executing implementation plans. Describes step-by-step process: load/review plan, execute tasks, complete development, with guidance on when to stop and ask for help.

sections.heading: When to Stop and Ask for Help

nodes.name: Finishing a Development Branch
nodes.summary: Skill guide for completing development branches. Covers verification, base branch determination, and options for merge locally, push/PR, keep as-is, or discard.

nodes.name: LICENSE.txt
nodes.summary: MIT License file for the frontend-design skill. Standard MIT license text.

nodes.name: Frontend Design
nodes.summary: Skill guide for frontend design. Provides design thinking and frontend aesthetics guidelines for creating production-grade interfaces.

nodes.name: Grill Me
nodes.summary: Skill guide for the grill-me interview process. Short skill file with minimal content, referencing the main review/interview workflow.

nodes.name: Receiving Code Review
nodes.summary: Skill guide for receiving code review feedback. Covers response patterns, handling unclear feedback, source-specific handling, YAGNI checks, and when to push back.

nodes.name: Code Reviewer
nodes.summary: Prompt template for the code review agent. Defines review checklist covering YAGNI, Karpathy principles, test quality, architecture, and concurrency with structured output format.

nodes.name: Requesting Code Review
nodes.summary: Skill guide for requesting code reviews. Describes when to request reviews (after major features, before merging), how to request with structured prompts, and integration with git workflows.

nodes.name: condition-based-waiting-example.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript example implementing condition-based waiting patterns for debugging. Provides waitForEvent, waitForEventCount, and waitForEventMatch functions using polling with timeout and predicate matching.

nodes.name: Test-Driven Development (TDD)
nodes.summary: Comprehensive TDD skill guide covering the Red-Green-Refactor cycle, good test practices, order importance, common rationalizations, red flags, and integration with debugging workflows.

sections.heading: Red Flags - STOP and Start Over

nodes.name: Testing Anti-Patterns
nodes.summary: Document describing five testing anti-patterns: testing mock behavior, test-only methods in production, mocking without understanding, incomplete mocks, and integration tests as afterthought.

nodes.name: _sync_all.py
nodes.summary: Python script for color palette synchronization. Provides color manipulation functions (hex/RGB conversion, luminance, blending, shifting) and rebuild pipelines for color and UI reasoning datasets from CSV sources.

nodes.name: core.py
nodes.summary: Core search engine for ui-ux-pro-max skill. Implements BM25 ranking algorithm, CSV-based search with multi-field tokenization, domain detection, and stack-specific search.

nodes.name: design_system.py
nodes.summary: Design system generator for ui-ux-pro-max skill. Generates comprehensive design systems with ASCII box and Markdown output formats, multi-domain search, intelligent style overrides, and page-specific detection.

nodes.name: search.py
nodes.summary: Search output formatter for ui-ux-pro-max skill. Provides formatted output rendering for search results.

nodes.name: UI/UX Pro Max
nodes.summary: Comprehensive UI/UX design skill covering 50+ styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 161 product types, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 10 stacks.

nodes.name: Using Git Worktrees
nodes.summary: Skill guide for managing git worktrees. Covers creation, switching, cleanup, smart directory selection, and safety verification for isolated development environments.

nodes.name: Using Superpowers
nodes.summary: Meta-skill guide for discovering and using available skills in the project. Establishes the protocol for Skill tool invocation before any response including clarifying questions.

nodes.name: Verification Before Completion
nodes.summary: Skill guide requiring verification before claiming work complete. Mandates running verification commands and confirming output before making success claims.

nodes.name: Plan Document Reviewer Prompt
nodes.summary: Prompt template for reviewing plan documents. Defines evaluation criteria for plan quality, completeness, feasibility, and alignment with project architecture.

nodes.name: Writing Plans
nodes.summary: Skill guide for writing implementation plans. Covers plan structure, task decomposition, verification steps, and integration with the executing-plans skill.

nodes.name: CLAUDE_MD_TESTING.md
nodes.summary: Example CLAUDE.md file for skill testing and evaluation. Demonstrates proper CLAUDE.md format and structure conventions.

反模式 5：写 E2E 时按 `{"data": {...}}` 解码响应

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: HTTP endpoints, route definitions, data transfer objects, request/response handling, and authentication middleware

layers.name: Application Layer
layers.description: Use cases, commands, and queries orchestrating domain logic for API key management, audit, endpoint config, identity, LLM proxy, model management, OAuth2, and session management

layers.name: Domain Layer
layers.description: Core domain entities, aggregates, repository interfaces, value objects, and domain services for API keys, conversations, identity, LLM proxy, model calls, OAuth2, and sessions

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Database models, DAOs, repositories, HTTP/SSE transport, cache layer, object storage, and service implementations for persistence and external communication

layers.name: Common & Configuration
layers.description: Shared constants, enums, error handling, configuration management (Viper), utility functions, JWT helpers, distributed locks, and linting tools

layers.name: Bootstrap & Cron
layers.description: Dependency injection container, router wiring, and scheduled background jobs for session deduplication, summarization, scoring, and purging

layers.name: CLI & Entry Point
layers.description: Command-line interface (Cobra), entry point (main.go), and Go module definition

layers.name: Frontend
layers.description: Next.js web application with React components, API client, authentication, and dashboard UI for API key, endpoint, model, and session management

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation including API docs, architecture specs, workflow plans, and markdown reference files

layers.name: DevOps & CI/CD

tour.title: Project Overview & Architecture
tour.description: aris-proxy-api is a production-grade Go backend that serves as an LLM API proxy gateway. It provides authentication, session management, API key management, model routing, request auditing, and a Next.js management dashboard — all organized in clean architecture layers with dependency injection.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point with Cobra
tour.description: The application bootstraps through main.go using the Cobra CLI framework. Commands for server startup, database migration, static lint analysis, and object storage management are wired in cmd/root.go and dispatched to subcommands like cmd/server.go and cmd/database.go.

tour.title: Dependency Injection Container
tour.description: All dependencies — database connections, Redis, HTTP clients, goroutine pools, JWT singletons, cron schedulers, use cases, and middleware — are wired together in internal/bootstrap/container.go using uber-go/dig. internal/bootstrap/router.go registers all Huma and Fiber routes after container construction.

tour.title: HTTP API Layer, Routing & Middleware
tour.description: Fiber v2 serves as the HTTP engine with Huma for OpenAPI-compliant route registration. Routes are defined per-resource in internal/router/, with a comprehensive middleware stack in internal/middleware/ providing JWT authentication, API key auth, CORS, rate limiting, trace ID injection, compression, panic recovery, and permission validation.

tour.title: Handlers — Thin HTTP Controllers
tour.description: Handlers in internal/handler/ are thin wrappers that receive Huma requests, delegate to application use cases, and wrap responses. Each resource (user, session, apikey, endpoint, model, audit, openai, anthropic, oauth2, token, ping) has a dedicated handler file.

tour.title: Application Layer — CQRS Use Cases
tour.description: Business operations follow CQRS with Commands (mutations) and Queries (reads) in internal/application/. Each domain module — apikey, endpoint, model, session, audit, identity, oauth2, llmproxy — separates write commands from read queries, keeping the orchestration logic decoupled from both HTTP and infrastructure concerns.

tour.title: Domain Layer — Core Business Entities
tour.description: The domain layer holds aggregates (endpoint, model, session, modelcall), value objects (token pairs, call latency, upstream credentials), repository interfaces, and domain services. The endpoint resolver in llmproxy/service/resolver.go is a key domain service that matches upstream provider endpoints to LLM proxy requests.

tour.title: LLM Proxy Pipeline — The Core Feature
tour.description: The LLM proxy in internal/application/llmproxy/ is the centerpiece: it discovers endpoints from the resolver, converts incoming requests (OpenAI chat completions, Anthropic messages) to the appropriate upstream format via converters, handles SSE streaming, stores session records, and manages tool call passthrough.

tour.title: Data Transfer Objects & API Contracts
tour.description: DTOs in internal/dto/ define request and response schemas for every HTTP endpoint. Full protocol compatibility is maintained for OpenAI (chat, response, tools) and Anthropic (messages, count_tokens) APIs. These structs are registered with Huma for auto-generated OpenAPI V3 documentation and validation.

tour.title: Infrastructure: Database, Transport & Cache
tour.description: PostgreSQL models and DAOs in internal/infrastructure/database/ handle persistence with GORM. The transport layer (openai.go, anthropic.go) manages HTTP/SSE communication with upstream LLM providers. Redis caches session details and shares, while MinIO/COS provides object storage for audio and file uploads.

tour.title: Configuration, Constants & Error Handling
tour.description: Application-wide constants live in internal/common/constant/ (Redis keys, HTTP headers, error codes, route patterns, SSE events). Enums in internal/enum/ and internal/common/enum/ define provider types, roles, tool choices, and more. The ierr package provides structured error wrapping with sentinels for consistent business error handling.

tour.title: Identity, OAuth2 & Security Infrastructure
tour.description: JWT token management in internal/infrastructure/jwt/ handles access and refresh token lifecycle. OAuth2 providers (GitHub, Google) are implemented in internal/infrastructure/oauth2/ with a common abstraction layer. Distributed locks in internal/lock/ prevent concurrent session processing, and internal/logger/ sends structured logs to CLS.

tour.title: Frontend — Next.js Management Dashboard
tour.description: A Next.js 16 App Router frontend in web/ provides the admin dashboard for API key, endpoint, model, and session management. It uses shadcn/ui components, lucide-react icons, and Tailwind v4 styling. The api-client.ts layer handles authenticated HTTP calls with automatic token refresh, and the auth-context manages login state.

tour.title: DevOps, CI/CD & Tests
tour.description: Docker Compose configurations (full, single, dev) spin up PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO, and the API server. Kubernetes manifests in k8s/ support production deployment. GitHub Actions build multi-architecture Docker images. Unit tests (60+) and E2E tests (10+) cover every layer from domain logic through SSE streaming and API proxy behavior.
